Cool and Dry to Warm and Dry

Last Updated Wed April 08 at 10:50AM by Ray Russell

During the next seven days, we transition from a cool Eastern U.S. trough to a warm Southeast ridge. The takeaway is “cool and dry to warm and dry.”

First, the cool… Cool air wedges down the east side of the Appalachians today, keeping temperatures noticeably lower. Valley frost is a good bet tonight. Temperatures begin to moderate Thursday afternoon, and that warming trend continues into early next week.

Forecast Discussion

Aloft, the next seven days produce a transition from an Eastern trough to a Southeast ridge. It’s cool transitioning to warm, with no significant rain in sight.

High pressure centered in New York today holds a cool wedge down the east side of the Appalachians. Wednesday is noticeably cooler than Tuesday, with some valley frost tonight.

That cool wedge gradually erodes beginning Thursday afternoon. We add about 8 degrees to afternoon temperatures Thursday and another 8 Friday.

A moisture-starved backdoor front approaches Saturday and stalls over us early Sunday. With it, we have a “barely worth mentioning” potential for a renegade afternoon shower Saturday. Temperatures continue to creep upward Saturday and Sunday.

Sunday afternoon into Monday, high pressure strengthens along the Southeast Coast, forcing the front back to the north. Temperatures really take off by Tuesday—the warmest day of this period.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for April 6, 2026

The first full week of April features some chilly conditions to start, but warming up by late week and during the weekend of the 11th-12th. Dry weather will be with us all week.

Apr 06, 2026
Easter Outlook

Easter Sunday will feature a rainy start to the day, but expect some afternoon brightening as clouds begin to decrease. Cooler air will arrive behind a frontal passage.

Next week looks seasonably cool and generally sunny. Frost and freeze concerns will be with us, particularly early Wednesday.
